revision.text:
[[ja:伊予親王]][[zh:伊予親王]] {{Infobox person|birth_date=0783|death_date={{death date|0807|12|14}}|death_place={{ill|Kawara-dera|qid=Q3194342|1=Kawara-dera}}|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|children={{ill|Takae-ō|qid=Q11670529|1=Takae-ō}}; {{ill|Tsugue-ō|qid=Q29410849|1=Tsugue-ō}}|father={{ill|Emperor Kanmu|qid=Q314846|1=Emperor Kanmu}}|mother={{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|qid=Q11623103|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko}}|relatives={{ill|Asahara-naishinnō|qid=Q11092514|1=Asahara-naishinnō}}; {{ill|Asuka-shinnō|qid=Q13292468|1=Asuka-shinnō}}; {{ill|Ate-naishinnō|qid=Q11450406|1=Ate-naishinnō}}; {{ill|Emperor Heizei|qid=Q353558|1=Emperor Heizei}}; {{ill|Emperor Junna|qid=Q374136|1=Emperor Junna}}; {{ill|Emperor Saga|qid=Q316654|1=Emperor Saga}}; {{ill|Fujii-shinnō|qid=Q11620866|1=Fujii-shinnō}}; {{ill|Fuse-naishinnō|qid=Q11480761|1=Fuse-naishinnō}}; {{ill|Ito-naishinnō|qid=Q11380927|1=Princess Ito}}; {{ill|Kaya-shinnō|qid=Q11634959|1=Kaya-shinnō}}; {{ill|Kazurawara-shinnō|qid=Q11620877|1=Kazurawara-shinnō}}; {{ill|Koshi-naishinnō|qid=Q11669919|1=Koshi-naishinnō}}; {{ill|Kōzu-naishinnō|qid=Q11671787|1=Kōzu-naishinnō}}; {{ill|Manta-shinnō|qid=Q11353795|1=Manta-shinnō}}; {{ill|Nakano-shinnō|qid=Q11378621|1=Prince Nakano}}; {{ill|Sakamoto-shinnō|qid=Q18193371|1=Sakamoto-shinnō}}; {{ill|Sami-shinnō|qid=Q13292202|1=Sami-shinnō}}; {{ill|Sugawara-naishinnō|qid=Q15839374|1=Sugawara-naishinnō}}; {{ill|Yoshimine no Yasuyo|qid=Q11614402|1=Yoshimine no Yasuyo}}; {{ill|Ōyake-naishinnō|qid=Q11434304|1=Ōyake-naishinnō}}}} {{Infobox Imperial Family | name = Prince Iyo | image = | image_size = | caption = | era = Late [[Nara period]] - Early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|qid=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 2 ({{ill|783|en|783|qid=Q30557|lt=783|1=783}})? | death_date = [[Daidō (Japan)|Daidō]] 2, {{ill|12th day of the 11th month (lunar calendar)|ja|11月12日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/12th day of the 11th month (lunar calendar)|1=November 12 (Lunisolar)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q840309}} ({{ill|December 14, 807|ja|807th year 12月14日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/December 14, 807|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}) | posthumous_name = | Buddhist_name = | burial_place = {{ill|Yamashiro Province|ja|Yamashiro Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, Kyohata Tomb ({{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|en|Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|qid=Q1197771|lt=Fushimi-ku, Kyoto|1=Fushimi-ku, Kyoto}}) | Rank = {{ill|Third Rank|ja|三品|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Third Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 品位 (位階)|1=Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q240268}}, [[Nakatsukasa Minister|Minister of Central Affairs]], [[Posthumous title|posthumously promoted]] to {{ill|First Rank|ja|一品親王|1=First Rank|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First Rank|13=User:Immanuelle/First Rank|qq=}} | parents = Father: {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|qid=Q314846|lt=Emperor Kanmu|1=Emperor Kanmu}}, Mother: {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623103}} | siblings = {{ill|Emperor Heizei|en|Emperor Heizei|qid=Q353558|lt=Emperor Heizei|1=Emperor Heizei}}, {{ill|Princess Asahara|ja|朝原内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Asahara|1=Asahara-naishinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11092514}}, {{ill|Nagaoka no Okinari|ja|長岡岡成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagaoka no Okinari|1=Nagaoka no Okanari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18234145}}, '''Prince Iyo''', {{ill|Yoshimine no Yasuyo|ja|良岑安世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yoshimine no Yasuyo|1=Yoshimine no Yasuyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11614402}}, {{ill|Emperor Saga|en|Emperor Saga|qid=Q316654|lt=Emperor Saga|1=Emperor Saga}}, {{ill|Emperor Junna|en|Emperor Junna|qid=Q374136|lt=Emperor Junna|1=Emperor Junna}}, {{ill|Prince Kazurahara|ja|葛原親王|1=Kazurawara-shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kazurahara|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11620877}}, {{ill|Princess Ōyake|ja|大宅内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Ōyake|1=Ōyake-naishinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11434304}}, {{ill|Princess Takatsu|ja|高津内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Takatsu|1=Kōzu-naishinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11671787}}, {{ill|Prince Manta|ja|万多親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Manta|1=Manta-shinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11353795}}, {{ill|Princess Koshi|ja|高志内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Koshi|1=Koshi-naishinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11669919}}, {{ill|Prince Asuka|ja|明日香親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Asuka|1=Asuka-shinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q13292468}}, {{ill|Prince Nakano|ja|仲野親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Nakano|qq=}}, {{ill|Prince Sami|ja|佐味親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Sami|1=Sami-shinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q13292202}}, {{ill|Prince Sakamoto|ja|坂本親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Sakamoto|1=Sakamoto-shinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q18193371}}, {{ill|Prince Kayō|ja|賀陽親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kayō|1=Kaya-shinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11634959}}, {{ill|Princess Fuse|ja|布勢内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Fuse|1=Fuse-naishinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11480761}}, {{ill|Prince Katsuragi|ja|葛井親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Katsuragi|1=Fujii-shinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11620866}}, {{ill|Princess Anchoku|ja|安勅内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Anchoku|1=Ate-naishinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11450406}}, {{ill|Princess Garaku|ja|賀楽内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Garaku|1=Kara-naishinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q73111653}}, {{ill|Princess Sugawara|ja|菅原内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Sugawara|1=Sugawara-naishinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q15839374}}, {{ill|Princess Kananami|ja|甘南美内親王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Kananami|1=Kannabi-naishinnō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q41699124}}, {{ill|Princess Ito|en|Princess Ito|qid=Q11380927|lt=Princess Ito|1=Princess Ito}}, and others | spouse = | children = {{ill|Prince Tsugue|ja|継枝王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tsugue|1=Tsugue-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q29410849}}, {{ill|Prince Takae|ja|高枝王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Takae|1=Takae-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11670529}}, a daughter | notable = }} '''Prince Iyo''' (伊予親王) was the third prince of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|qid=Q314846|lt=Emperor Kanmu|1=Emperor Kanmu}} (though some sources differ). His mother was {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623103}}, the daughter of the Minister of the Right, {{ill|Fujiwara no Korekimi|en|Fujiwara no Korekimi|qid=Q11623313|lt=Fujiwara no Korekimi|1=Fujiwara no Korekimi}}. Holding the Rank of {{ill|Third Rank|ja|三品|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Third Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 品位 (位階)|1=Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q240268}}, {{ill|Ministry of the Center|ja|中務卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ministry of the Center|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中務省|1=Ministry of the Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6867690}}, and posthumously promoted to {{ill|First Rank|ja|一品|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 一品親王}}, he was implicated in a rebellion against {{ill|Emperor Heizei|en|Emperor Heizei|qid=Q353558|lt=Emperor Heizei|1=Emperor Heizei}} and, along with his mother, was confined and forced to commit suicide by starvation ({{ill|Rebellion of Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王の変|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rebellion of Prince Iyo|1=incident of Imperial Prince Iyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11378832}}). == Career == In {{ill|Enryaku|en|Enryaku|qid=Q1373176|lt=Enryaku|1=Enryaku}} 11 ({{ill|792|en|792|qid=Q31116|lt=792|1=792}}), Prince Iyo celebrated his coming of age. He was deeply loved by his father, {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|qid=Q314846|lt=Emperor Kanmu|1=Emperor Kanmu}}Tokuei Mezaki, "Offerings in Early Heian Period," *Heian Cultural History*, Ōfūsha, 1968., and the emperor often visited Iyo's residence during hunting expeditions*Shoku Nihongi*, Enryaku 17, 13th day of the 8th month; Enryaku 22, 19th day of the 8th month and 27th day; Enryaku 23, 20th day of the 2nd month and 11th day of the 5th month.. In Enryaku 25 ({{ill|806|en|806|qid=Q30021|lt=806|1=806}}), upon the ascension of {{ill|Emperor Heizei|en|Emperor Heizei|qid=Q353558|lt=Emperor Heizei|1=Emperor Heizei}}, Iyo's maternal uncle, {{ill|Fujiwara no Oyotomo|ja|藤原雄友|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Oyotomo|1=Fujiwara no Otomo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623635}}, was appointed {{ill|Dainagon|en|Dainagon|qid=Q1354706|lt=Dainagon|1=Dainagon}}, taking the second-highest position in the {{ill|Daijō-kan|en|Daijō-kan|qid=Q1192939|lt=Daijō-kan|1=Daijō-kan}}. Iyo himself was appointed {{ill|Nakatsukasa Minister|ja|中務卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakatsukasa Minister|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 中務省|1=Ministry of the Center|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6867690}} and {{ill|Dazai Governor|ja|大宰帥|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dazai Governor|1=Dazai no Sochi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11434484}}, securing his position as a leading member of the royal family. In Daidō 2 ({{ill|807|en|807|qid=Q30016|lt=807|1=807}}), during an imperial visit to {{ill|Shinsenen|en|Shinsenen|qid=Q11590468|lt=Shinsenen|1=Shinsenen}}, he presented offerings and joined the festivities, maintaining an outwardly cordial relationship with Emperor Heizei*Shoku Nihongi*, Daidō 2, 20th day of the 5th month.. [[File:Tomb of Prince Iyo, entrance.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Prince Iyo's Kyohata Tomb
({{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto|1=Kyoto}})}}]] However, in October of the same year, {{ill|Fujiwara no Munenari|ja|藤原宗成|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Munenari|1=Fujiwara no Munenari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623161}} reportedly attempted to incite Prince Iyo to rebellion, a fact reported by Fujiwara no Oyotomo to {{ill|Minister of the Right|en|Minister of the Right|qid=Q1351174|lt=Minister of the Right|1=Minister of the Right}}, {{ill|Fujiwara no Uchimaro|en|Fujiwara no Uchimaro|qid=Q11623088|lt=Fujiwara no Uchimaro|1=Fujiwara no Uchimaro}}. Despite Iyo's declaration that Munenari alone was guilty*Shoku Nihongi*, Daidō 2, 28th day of the 10th month., Munenari accused Iyo of being the mastermind. Iyo was then arrested by 140 soldiers under {{ill|Abe no Eniwa|ja|安倍兄雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abe no Eniwa|1=Abe no Eio|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11450293}} and {{ill|Kose no Yatari|ja|巨勢野足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose no Yatari|1=Kose no Notari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11479784}}*Shoku Nihongi*, Daidō 2, 30th day of the 10th month. and imprisoned at {{ill|Kawara-dera|en|Kawara-dera|qid=Q3194342|lt=Kawaradera Temple|1=Kawara-dera}} with his mother. Both were deprived of food and committed suicide by poisoning on the 12th day of the 11th month*Shoku Nihongi*, Daidō 2, 12th day of the 11th month.. Their deaths evoked great sympathy among the people. Later, Prince Iyo's innocence was recognized. During the reign of {{ill|Emperor Junna|en|Emperor Junna|qid=Q374136|lt=Emperor Junna|1=Emperor Junna}}, in {{ill|Kōnin (era)|en|Kōnin (era)|qid=Q1373070|lt=Kōnin|1=Kōnin (era)}} 14 ({{ill|823|en|823|qid=Q29944|lt=823|1=823}}), he and his mother were posthumously reinstated. In {{ill|Jōwa|en|Jōwa|qid=Q3577279|lt=Jōwa|1=Jōwa}} 6 ({{ill|839|en|839|qid=Q23874|lt=839|1=839}}), he was posthumously elevated to {{ill|First Rank|ja|贈一品|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/First Rank|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. == Official Appointments == [[:en:※|※]] Based on the {{ill|Rikkokushi|en|Rikkokushi|qid=Q1151168|lt=Rikkokushi|1=Rikkokushi}}. * Enryaku 11 (792): February 15 – Coming of age * Enryaku 23 (804): February 20 – Granted {{ill|Third Rank|ja|三品|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Third Rank|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 品位 (位階)|1=Rank|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q240268}} and appointed Minister of Ceremonies ({{ill|Shikibu-kyō|ja|式部卿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikibu-kyō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 式部省|1=Shikibu-shō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3727885}}). September 3 – Granted 53 chō of wasteland in {{ill|Gamo District, Ōmi Province|ja|近江国蒲生郡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gamo District, Ōmi Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. * Enryaku 25 (806): May 9 – Appointed Nakatsukasa Minister and Dazai Governor * Daidō 2 (807): November 11 – Stripped of princely Rank * Kōnin 14 (823): July 25 – Reinstated (Third Rank) * Unknown date: Posthumously promoted to Second Rank * Jōwa 6 (839): September 5 – Posthumously elevated to First Rank == Genealogy == * Father: {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|qid=Q314846|lt=Emperor Kanmu|1=Emperor Kanmu}} * Mother: {{ill|Fujiwara no Yoshiko|ja|藤原吉子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Yoshiko|1=Fujiwara no Yoshiko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11623103}} (daughter of {{ill|Fujiwara no Korekimi|en|Fujiwara no Korekimi|qid=Q11623313|lt=Fujiwara no Korekimi|1=Fujiwara no Korekimi}}) * Children (mother unknown): ** Son: {{ill|Prince Tsugue|ja|継枝王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Tsugue|1=Tsugue-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q29410849}} (d. 846) ** Son: {{ill|Prince Takae|ja|高枝王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Takae|1=Takae-ō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11670529}} (802–858) ** Daughter: {{ill|Princess Yoshioka|ja|吉岡女王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Princess Yoshioka|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}?Sakuragi Jun, "Background of the Iyo Rebellion—Focus on the Prince's Children and Literature," *Ancient Culture* 56-3 (2004). {{ill|Fujiwara no Tamayoshi|ja|藤原為世|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Tamayoshi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 二条為世|1=Nijō Tameyo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11371664}}, ancestor of the {{ill|Iyo Tachibana clan|ja|伊予橘氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Tachibana clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 橘氏 (伊予国)|1=Iyo Tachibana clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11544307}} and {{ill|Ochi clan|ja|越智氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi clan|qq=}}, is said to be Prince Iyo's son. According to one theory, {{ill|Emperor Saga|en|Emperor Saga|qid=Q316654|lt=Emperor Saga|1=Emperor Saga}} ordered that Tamayoshi be treated as a prince and granted him the surname {{ill|Fujiwara clan|en|Fujiwara clan|qid=Q641628|lt=Fujiwara|1=Fujiwara clan}}. == Notes == {{Reflist|2}} == See Also == * {{ill|Goryō|en|Goryō|qid=Q3273210|lt=Goryō faith|1=Goryō}} * {{ill|Kamigoryō Shrine|ja|上御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamigoryō Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Shimo-Goryō Shrine|ja|下御霊神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimo-Goryō Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Sakuraba Daimyōjin|ja|桜葉大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakuraba Daimyōjin|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Worshipped as a retainer of {{ill|Tenjin (kami)|en|Tenjin (kami)|qid=Q1753428|lt=Tenjin|1=Tenjin (kami)}}, considered identical to {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} at {{ill|Tenmangū|en|Tenmangū|qid=Q798838|lt=Tenmangū shrines|1=Tenmangū}}. * {{ill|Prince Kōsei|ja|興世王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Kōsei|1=okiyoou|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11613329}} – Said to be a descendant of Prince Iyo. * {{ill|Kyohata Tomb 2|ja|黄金塚2号墳|1=Koganezuka No. 2 Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyohata Tomb 2|13=User:Immanuelle/Koganezuka No. 2 Kofun|qq=}} ...omb" ({{ill|Koganezuka No. 2 Kofun|lt=Koganezuka No. 2 Kofun|lt=Kohata Tomb|ja|巨幡墓|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kohata Tomb|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 黄金塚2号墳}}), the burial place of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}} (the third prince of {{ill|Emperor Kanmu|en|Emperor Kanmu|qid=Q314846|lt=Emperor Kanmu|1=Emperor Kanmu}}). ...as the Kohata Tomb, believed to be the burial place of {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}} (d. 807), based on the designation by the {{ill|Tachibana clan (kuge)|en|Tachibana clan (kuge)|qid=Q1500519|lt=Imperial Household Agency ... 6 KB (755 words) - 21:06, 15 January * [[Ōno clan|1=Tachibana clan (kuge)}} ...chibana Clan]], Fujiwara Clan, or descendants of Prince {{ill|Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}. ... 3 KB (418 words) - 01:44, 13 January * [[Tamayori Gozen]] ...{{ill|Ato no Ōtari|ja|阿刀大足|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ato no Ōtari|qq=}}, who served as a tutor to {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}. Her family is believed to have been influential and resided near the cur ... 4 KB (498 words) - 00:09, 15 January * [[Yoki Tenman Shrine|Yoiki Tenma Shrine]]''' Sakuraha Shrine (祭神: {{ill|Iyo Shinnō|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}) ... 8 KB (948 words) - 07:29, 15 January * [[Goryō Shrine (Nara)|Goryō Shrine (Nara City)]]''' Deities: {{ill|Prince Iyo|ja|伊予親王|1=Iyo Shinnō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Iyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shinnō|qq=}}, {{ill|Tachibana no Hayanari|en|Tachibana no Hayanari|qid=Q1062686|lt=Tachibana no Hayanari|1=Tachibana no Hayanari}}, {{ill|Fumihara no Miyatamaro|ja|文室宮田麻呂|1=Fun'ya no Miyatamaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11499944}}... 9 KB (1,099 words) - 01:11, 15 January * [[Emperor Kanmu]] '''Second Son: Imperial Prince Iyo (伊予親王; 783–807) ... 29 KB (3,888 words) - 00:42, 13 January '''Prince Iyo''' {{translated page|ja|伊予親王|version=104506740|comment=Imported full ja history}}qq=simple|qq=User:Immanuelle/Fumihara no Miyatamaro|qq=}} {{draft categories||qq=draft}} [[ja:伊予親王]] [[ja:%27%27%27%E4%BC%8A%E4%BA%88%E8%A6%AA%E7%8E%8B%27%27%27]] [[zh:伊予親王]] {{wikidata link|Q11378831}} [[Category:807 deaths]] [[Category:8th-century births]] [[Category:Children of Emperor Kammu]] [[Category:Deified Japanese people in the Goryō faith]] [[Category:Japanese Royalty in the 8th Century]] [[Category:Japanese Royalty in the 9th Century]] [[Category:Joint suicides]] [[Category:Princes of Japan in the Heian period]] [[Category:Shinnō (Imperial Princes of Japan)]] [[Category:Suicides in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
